page: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the E2 family. It features 8 virtual CPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7B12 processor, utilizing multithreading with 2 threads per core. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The processor includes L1d cache of 32 KB, L2 cache of 0.5 MB, and L3 cache of 16 MB, supporting instruction sets such as AVX, AES, SHA_NI, BMI2, and FMA, while lacking AVX-512 and AMX extensions.

Benchmark data reveals a mix of strong single-core capabilities and average multi-core performance. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng is in the top 25% with a score of 2687.38, whereas multi-core CPU performance falls into the middle 50% at 10752.43. Memory performance represents a notable bottleneck, as evidenced by a weak PassMark memory score in the bottom 25% of 1275.42 and a weak multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 33.88 GB/sec. Conversely, memory bandwidth remains average, reaching 507.5 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 73.75 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the e2-highcpu-8 offers a high density of dedicated vCPUs relative to its memory footprint, making it a viable option for compute-bound workloads that do not require large memory allocations. It achieves average performance across several utility benchmarks, including Redis SET operations at 8.24 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 8.79 GB/sec. This resource profile is suited for tasks such as software compilation, web serving, text processing, and lightweight single-threaded data processing, though it is constrained for memory-intensive databases or heavy multi-threaded decompression tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 compute-focused instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B12 processor with 4 physical cores. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU execution, placing in the top 25% in stress-ng tests, while multi-core performance and memory operations generally fall into average or weak categories, such as a weak PassMark memory score. With a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1.0 GB per core, this instance provides a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy, memory-light workloads. It is suited for web serving, software compilation, and text processing, but is limited for memory-intensive database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 instance with 8 vCPUs and 8 GB RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B12 CPU, it offers strong single-core performance but limited memory capacity.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-8 features 8 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, delivering strong single-core compute with low memory density.
tagline: A compute-focused x86_64 instance offering high vCPU density and strong single-core performance for memory-light, CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B12 processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores.
Configured with 8.0 GB of RAM, providing a low 1.0 GB per vCPU ratio.
Strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% of stress-ng benchmarks.
Lacks local storage, bundled GPUs, and complimentary public IPv4 addresses.
Suited for web serving, software compilation, and text processing workloads.
Constrained by weak multi-threaded decompression and low memory performance.
